div.h1: Epidemic Spreading Model (SIR) Applied to Gossip

div.p: P(susceptible after R rounds) ≈ (1 - k/N)^(I × R) where: k = fanout (3) N = cluster size (10) I = infected nodes spreading R = number of rounds For N=10, k=3: log₃(10) ≈ 2.1 rounds (theoretical) ~3-4 rounds (practical with variance)

Round 0: I=1 → spreads to k=3 new nodes Round 1: I=3 → spreads to k×3=9 potential (6 unique) Round 2: I=6 → spreads to remaining 4 nodes Round 3: I=0 → all nodes recovered Key: Each infected node contacts k random peers → Exponential growth of informed nodes → log_k(N) rounds to full dissemination
